destinations.evidence: The nodebuilder Makefile constructs GIT_COMMIT from the local reth port checkout short SHA and the nodebuilder checkout short SHA separated by a hyphen.
destinations.snippet:
RETH_INTERNAL_COMMIT := $(shell cd ../ritual-reth-internal-v2.2.0-port && git rev-parse --short=7 HEAD)
NODEBUILDER_COMMIT := $(shell git rev-parse --short=7 HEAD)
GIT_COMMIT := $(RETH_INTERNAL_COMMIT)-$(NODEBUILDER_COMMIT)

equivalence_argument: The nodebuilder repository owns image/version metadata assembly, so the invariant maps to its Makefile rather than to the port crate code.
notes: The local path references the v2.2.0 port checkout explicitly.
rationale: The commit subject says git calculation should include reth too, and the diff adds separate short SHA reads for `../ritual-reth-internal` and the current repo before concatenating them. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: The Makefile `GIT_COMMIT` value is formed as `<ritual-reth-internal short sha>-<nodebuilder short sha>` rather than only the nodebuilder repository's commit.
suggested_falsification_tests: Run `make` target or evaluate `GIT_COMMIT` with both repos present and assert it equals the short SHA of `../ritual-reth-internal`, a hyphen, and the short SHA of nodebuilder.
summary: Build commit metadata includes both repo SHAs.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Evaluate the Makefile variables with both repos present and assert GIT_COMMIT is <reth-port-short-sha>-<nodebuilder-short-sha>.
title: Build commit includes both repos
